forced_causal_chains.topic: bottle ageing, sediment formation, and tertiary character

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A red wine with sufficient tannin, pigment, and structural components is aged in bottle over an extended period (years to decades)

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Tannin molecules and anthocyanin pigments polymerise (join together into larger molecules) and eventually precipitate out of solution; the wine's structure gradually softens as free tannins decrease

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Sediment (dark, gritty deposit) forms in the bottle; the wine develops tertiary aromas (leather, earth, dried fruit, mushroom, tobacco); tannins become softer and more integrated

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: A structured red wine, through bottle ageing, sees tannins and pigments polymerise, forming sediment and developing tertiary aromas (leather, earth, dried fruit) over time.

forced_causal_chains.sat_relevance: Tertiary aromas and soft tannins signal bottle development on the SAT. A wine showing tertiary character has aged; readiness to drink should be assessed from tannin integration, fruit condition, and balance.
